function_explanation: Applies the MRI forward operator to transform an image into masked k-space data.
function_docstring:
Applies the MRI forward operator to transform an image into masked k-space data.

Args:
    x (torch.Tensor): A tensor representing the input image with shape (Batch, Channels, Height, Width).
    maps (torch.Tensor): A tensor representing the coil sensitivity maps with shape (Batch, Coils, Height, Width).
    mask (torch.Tensor): A tensor representing the k-space mask with shape (Batch, Coils, Height, Width).

Returns:
    torch.Tensor: A tensor containing the masked k-space data with shape (Batch, Coils, Height, Width, 2), where the last dimension represents the real and imaginary parts.
usage_example:
import torch

# Example tensors
x = torch.rand((B, 2, H, W), dtype=torch.float64)  # Input image
maps = torch.rand((B, C, H, W), dtype=torch.complex128)  # Coil sensitivity maps
mask = torch.rand((B, C, H, W), dtype=torch.complex128)  # k-space mask

# Apply the forward operator
masked_kspace = forward_operator(x, maps, mask)
print(masked_kspace.shape)  # Should output: (B, C, H, W, 2)
